images.hook: A mythologized threat actor portrait that makes ransomware feel like a sovereign nation-state, triggering fear and fascination in equal measure.
images.caption:
They didn't just build ransomware. They built a cartel.

DragonForce debuted on BreachForums on December 6, 2023 — and within 18 months had claimed 363 victims across 30+ countries, absorbed RansomHub, and ran a franchise model giving affiliates 80% of every ransom paid.

This isn't a hacker group. This is organized cybercrime with brand standards.

Swipe to decode how they operate. 🐉

#DragonForce #Ransomware #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #RaaS
images.prompt:
CINEMATIC CYBERSECURITY PORTRAIT — aspect ratio 4:5 — vertical format for LinkedIn feed.

TOP STRIP: A sleek matte-black banner burned across the very top of the image, 10% of total height. Inside it, in sharp sans-serif white uppercase tracking: 'CASE STUDY: DRAGONFORCE RANSOMWARE CARTEL'. Directly beneath on a slightly thinner crimson sub-strip in lighter-weight font: 'Image 01 — Identity & Brand — The Circuit Dragon'.

MAIN VISUAL: A massive, hyper-detailed dragon constructed entirely from glowing circuit board traces, fiber optic threads, and cascading green-on-black hexadecimal data streams — rendered as a god-like entity rising from the center of a dark server room floor. The dragon's scales are individually etched PCB panels with soldered chips visible. Its eyes are twin CRT monitors displaying a skull-and-crosspile ransomware splash screen. Its wings are unfolding server blade racks that creak open with volumetric steam exhaust. The dragon's chest glows with an internal bioluminescent crimson-orange core — like a cooling server overloaded to meltdown.

BURNED-IN TEXT ELEMENTS: Etched directly into the dragon's left wing in glowing amber LED characters: 'FOUNDED: DEC 6 2023 — BREACHFORUMS'. Etched into the right wing: 'CARTEL ANNOUNCED: MAR 19 2025'. Burned into a cracked floor tile beneath the dragon's claw: 'VICTIMS: 363 CONFIRMED | 30+ COUNTRIES'. Across the dragon's chest plate as a branded emblem: 'DRAGONFORCE RaaS — AFFILIATE SPLIT: 80/20'. Along the dragon's tail coiling into the lower left, scrolling text reads: 'ChaCha8 | BYOVD | truesight.sys | rentdrv2.sys'. A glowing red timestamp in the upper-right corner of the scene reads: '2023-12-06 00:00:00 UTC — BREACH FORUMS POST #1'. On the server racks in the background, small LED status panels read: 'NODES ACTIVE: 363' and 'REGION: US 52% | UK 12% | AU 6%'.

BACKGROUND: A cathedral-scale dark data center — black basalt floors reflecting crimson light, infinite server rack corridors receding into fog, ceiling lost in darkness. Volumetric god-rays of deep crimson and electric gold pierce downward from unseen skylights, illuminating the dragon from above and slightly behind — a halo of corrupted light. The room smells of burning silicon (implied by heat-shimmer effects).

BOTTOM STRIP: A full-width matte-black strip across the bottom 10% of the image. Burned in sharp, cinematic white serif font with crimson accent on key words: '363 VICTIMS. 30 COUNTRIES. ONE CARTEL. DRAGONFORCE DIDN'T HACK THE SYSTEM — THEY FRANCHISED IT.'

COLOR PALETTE: Abyssal black #0A0A0F background | Dragon circuit glow #00FF41 matrix green | Core heat #FF3B1F crimson-orange | Gold accent #FFB800 | Steel grey #2C2C3A for server racks | Pure white #F5F5F5 for text | Deep crimson #8B0000 for sub-strips.

LIGHTING: Dramatic single-source volumetric spotlight from directly overhead, casting the dragon in heroic-villain chiaroscuro. Secondary rim light from below in deep crimson (#8B0000), simulating floor-level heat vents. Tertiary fill from matrix green screen glow from surrounding server walls. Lens flare on dragon eye-screens.

STYLE: Hyper-detailed digital oil painting meets photorealistic 3D render — the aesthetic of a AAA video game cinematic combined with a National Geographic wildlife portrait of a apex predator. Ultra-sharp foreground, atmospheric depth-of-field in server corridor background. Cinematic 2.39:1 color grade even within portrait format.

images.topic: The Binary Signature — Ransom Note Decoded
images.hook: Recreating an actual ransomware moment — a lit screen, a locked world — triggers visceral recognition in every security professional who has ever gotten that 3am call.
images.caption:
The note appears. The clock starts.

DragonForce's ransomware encrypts with ChaCha8 — blazing fast, cross-platform, hitting Windows, Linux, and ESXi hypervisors simultaneously. By the time your EDR tries to respond, it's already dead — killed by vulnerable drivers truesight.sys and rentdrv2.sys via a BYOVD attack (T1562.001).

Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods. Three British retail giants. One summer. £300M+ in confirmed impact.

This is what the note looks like. This is what it costs.

#DragonForce #Ransomware #IncidentResponse #CyberThreat #DFIR
images.prompt:
CINEMATIC CYBERSECURITY FORENSIC SCENE — aspect ratio 4:5 — vertical format for LinkedIn feed.

TOP STRIP: Matte-black top banner, 10% height. Sharp all-caps white sans-serif: 'CASE STUDY: DRAGONFORCE RANSOMWARE CARTEL'. Crimson sub-strip below it in lighter tracking: 'Image 02 — The Binary Signature — Ransom Note Decoded'.

MAIN VISUAL: Extreme close-up of a large curved ultrawide monitor, slightly tilted at a 15-degree angle toward the viewer, filling 80% of the frame. The screen is the scene. On the screen: a meticulously designed ransomware splash page rendered as if it is the actual DragonForce ransom note — a stylized dragon emblem watermark faint behind the text, matte dark background, monospace terminal font throughout.

BURNED DIRECTLY INTO THE MONITOR SCREEN as part of its display content:
'YOUR FILES HAVE BEEN ENCRYPTED WITH ChaCha8 ALGORITHM'
'ENCRYPTION ID: DF-2025-0412-M&S-PROD-ESXi'
'FILES AFFECTED: 4,382,910'
'DEADLINE: 72:00:00 REMAINING'
'RANSOM: [REDACTED — CONTACT DARKWEB PORTAL]'
'EDR STATUS: KILLED — truesight.sys BYOVD EXPLOIT DEPLOYED'
'MITRE T1486 | T1562.001 | T1059.003'
'DO NOT CONTACT LAW ENFORCEMENT — WE WILL KNOW'
A blinking red cursor at the bottom of the note pulses.

ON THE PHYSICAL DESK SURFACE surrounding the monitor: a coffee mug knocked on its side, cold coffee pooling across a printed incident response playbook whose cover reads 'IR PLAYBOOK REV 3.1 — RANSOMWARE'. Scattered sticky notes read: 'CALL EXEC — 3:47AM', 'IS BACKUP CLEAN??', 'DO NOT PAY — LEGAL SAYS'. A physical USB drive labeled 'truesight.sys EVIDENCE — DO NOT TOUCH' sits in an evidence bag. A phone screen shows '47 MISSED CALLS'. A sticky note on the monitor bezel reads: 'ATTACK SURGE +212.5%'.

BACKGROUND: A darkened corporate SOC (Security Operations Center) at 3:47 AM — rows of dead monitors behind, the single active screen casting the only light in the room. Through a glass wall in the far background, blurred silhouettes of panicked executives crowd in a conference room, their outlines barely visible in emergency red lighting.

On a whiteboard in the background, barely readable in the dimness: 'VICTIMS: M&S | CO-OP | HARRODS — SUMMER 2025 | IMPACT £300M+' and 'PEAK: 35 ORGS — DEC 2025'.

A timestamp burned into the lower-left corner of the scene (not the monitor): '2025-04-12 03:47:22 UTC — INCIDENT DETECTED'.

BOTTOM STRIP: Full-width matte-black strip. Cinematic white uppercase serif with crimson highlight: 'BY THE TIME THE NOTE APPEARS, THE EDR IS ALREADY DEAD. ChaCha8 DOESN'T NEGOTIATE — IT ENCRYPTS.'

COLOR PALETTE: Near-black room #080810 | Monitor glow cold blue-white #D6E8FF | Ransom text amber #FFB347 | Deadline timer red #FF2D2D blinking | Evidence bag yellow #F5C518 | Emergency glow behind glass #8B0000 | Spilled coffee warm brown #4A2C0A.

LIGHTING: Single-source illumination — the monitor itself is the only light in the scene, casting cold blue-white light across the protagonist's face (face not shown, only foreground objects lit). Hard shadows behind all desk objects. Red emergency light bleeds through the glass wall from the distant conference room. A thin rim light from below the desk in deep red implies heating vents or under-desk LED strips.

STYLE: Hyper-photorealistic forensic crime scene photography aesthetic — the composition of a war photographer embedded in a ransomware incident. Shallow depth of field, monitor tack-sharp, background bokeh. Film grain overlay at 15%. Anamorphic lens horizontal flare across the monitor from a reflection.

images.hook: Attribution mystery visualized as a physical war room creates the intrigue of an unsolved thriller — security professionals share what they cannot yet answer.
images.caption:
No one can confirm where DragonForce actually comes from.

Malaysia? Russia? A hacktivist front that went commercial? The group publicly emerged on BreachForums on December 6, 2023 — and deliberately obscured every trail behind them.

They collaborated with Scattered Spider for vishing attacks. They absorbed RansomHub on April 8, 2025. They operate like a cartel board of directors — anonymous, disciplined, and ruthlessly effective.

The question mark is the point. Unattributed threat actors are the hardest to defend against.

#ThreatIntelligence #DragonForce #CyberAttribution #ScatteredSpider #CyberSecurity
images.prompt:
CINEMATIC CYBERSECURITY INVESTIGATION SCENE — aspect ratio 4:5 — vertical format for LinkedIn feed.

TOP STRIP: Matte-black top banner, 10% height. Bold white all-caps sans-serif: 'CASE STUDY: DRAGONFORCE RANSOMWARE CARTEL'. Crimson sub-strip: 'Image 03 — The Origin Nobody Can Confirm'.

MAIN VISUAL: A massive intelligence cork board — aged, weathered, dramatically lit — filling the entire frame. The board is covered in a complex conspiracy-style web of investigation: photographs, printed documents, sticky notes, red string connecting nodes, printed dark web screenshots, and physical evidence tags. The board tells the story of a failed attribution attempt. Every element is meticulously labeled with real-world details burned directly into the artwork.

BURNED-IN TEXT ELEMENTS ACROSS THE BOARD:

— A printed photograph in the upper-left, face replaced by a pixelated DragonForce dragon icon, labeled below: 'UNKNOWN OPERATOR — ALIAS: DRAGONFORCE ADMIN' with a red stamp: 'UNCONFIRMED'.

— A world map pinned to the board with red pins in: Malaysia, Russia, United Kingdom, United States. Red string connects all four pins to a central question mark made of red string. The map is labeled: 'ORIGIN: UNKNOWN — POSSIBLE NEXUS POINTS'.

— A printed BreachForums post screenshot labeled: 'FIRST SIGHTING: DEC 6 2023 — BREACHFORUMS — POST #7,441'.

— A printed document titled 'AFFILIATE AGREEMENT — DRAGONFORCE RaaS v2.1' with key lines visible: 'Revenue share: Affiliate 80% | Cartel 20%' and 'Platforms: Windows, Linux, ESXi'.

— A sticky note reading: 'SCATTERED SPIDER COLLABORATION — VISHING OPS CONFIRMED — T1566.004'.

— A printed timeline strip across the middle of the board: 'DEC 2023 → LAUNCH | MAR 19 2025 → CARTEL MODEL | APR 8 2025 → RANSOMHUB ABSORBED | DEC 2025 → 35 ORGS IN ONE MONTH'.

— A red-stamped document: 'VICTIMS: M&S | CO-OP | HARRODS | £300M+ IMPACT | SUMMER 2025'.

— MITRE ATT&CK codes on small index cards pinned around the board: 'T1486 — Data Encrypted for Impact', 'T1562.001 — BYOVD: truesight.sys + rentdrv2.sys', 'T1059.003 — Command Shell Execution', 'T1078 — Valid Accounts — Vishing Vector'.

— A sticky note with handwritten scrawl: 'HACKTIVIST COVER STORY?? OR GENUINE?' with multiple question marks.

— A printed bar chart pinned to the board showing attack surge: a bar labeled '2023-2024' at baseline, and a bar labeled 'DEC 2025' at 212.5% higher, in red. Labeled: 'ATTACK SURGE: +212.5%'.

— A geographic breakdown card: 'US: 52% | UK: 12% | AU: 6% | OTHER: 30%'.

— A large central red question mark made of physical string dominates the center of the board with a torn paper label beneath it: 'ATTRIBUTION: PENDING — CONFIDENCE: LOW'.

SCENE CONTEXT: The board is lit by a single forensic overhead worklight clamped to the top of the board. The foreground shows the edge of a metal folding table with: an empty coffee cup, a printed MITRE ATT&CK Navigator screenshot, and an open laptop showing a threat intelligence dashboard. The room beyond the board is pure darkness — the board is the only lit object in an otherwise black void, giving it an interrogation-room, war-room intensity.

On the floor in the lower corner, barely visible in shadow: a box labeled 'EVIDENCE — DRAGONFORCE — CASE #DF-2025-001 — OPEN'.

BOTTOM STRIP: Full-width matte-black strip. Cinematic serif font, white with crimson accent on key words: 'NO CONFIRMED ORIGIN. NO KNOWN FACES. 363 VICTIMS. THE MOST DANGEROUS THREAT ACTOR IS THE ONE YOU CANNOT NAME.'

COLOR PALETTE: Aged cork #C9A96E | Red string #CC0000 | Document cream #F0E8D8 | Forensic spotlight white #FFFFF0 | Shadow void #050507 absolute black | Warning stamp red #FF2222 | Sticky note yellow #FFE066 | MITRE card steel blue #3A5A8A.

LIGHTING: A single forensic clamp-light mounted top-center, casting hard directional light downward at a 10-degree angle across the entire board surface — creating deep shadows beneath every pinned document and string. The light has a warm tungsten quality (#FFF3CC) with a 15% warm vignette. Absolute darkness surrounds the board in all directions. Subtle dust particles visible in the light beam, suggesting an old, classified investigation room. No fill light — pure chiaroscuro drama.

STYLE: Ultra-photorealistic crime documentary aesthetic — the visual language of an FBI evidence board from a prestige TV thriller. Every paper has visible texture, every string casts a shadow, every pin catches a highlight. Wide-angle 24mm equivalent lens with slight barrel distortion to suggest the viewer is standing close to the board. No depth of field blur — the entire board is sharp. Film grain 20%.
